headline: The geopolitical threat to globalisation
summary: The FT’s chief economics commentator, Martin Wolf, discusses why geopolitics could be the biggest threat to globalisation
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the FT News Briefing podcast episode: ‘The geopolitical threat to globalisation’

Marc FilippinoGood morning from the Financial Times. Today is Thursday, November 24th, and this is your FT News Briefing.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

We’re doing something a little different for this episode. In today’s show, we’re going to be focusing on one big topic: the state of deglobalisation and how geopolitics is contributing to this process. The FT’s Martin Wolf will give us the breakdown. I’m Marc Filippino and this is a special episode of the FT News Briefing.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

There are concerns that the world is becoming a less global place. And what I mean by that is that more and more countries are pulling back on global trade. And there are a lot of examples that back up that theory. Donald Trump started a trade war with China. The Covid-19 pandemic put supply chains to the test. And the war in Ukraine has gotten a lot of countries reconsidering how dependent they shou
